summary: The Sacramento County Department of Airports is seeking a qualified vendor to provide a comprehensive, fully supported cloud-based Project Management Software solution to manage active construction projects across all four of its airport facilities, including Sacramento International Airport (SMF), and to support its Capital Improvement Planning (CIP) process. The technical scope encompasses software licensing with unlimited user access, system installation and configuration, customization of workflows for RFIs, submittals, change orders, budget tracking, schedule management, and reporting dashboards, as well as integration with existing County systems including ERP, GIS, and Single Sign-On platforms via APIs. The contract is structured with an initial four-year term and two additional two-year extension options for a possible total of eight years, with an anticipated agreement effective date in July/August 2026 and evaluation based on a 100-point scoring system covering service support capabilities, implementation plan, company qualifications, relevant public sector experience, and fee schedule. This initiative will replace the Department's current Excel-based and Crystal Reports tracking processes, reducing manual effort and improving accuracy across all project phases while enabling portfolio-level visibility, prioritization, and long-term capital forecasting. Proposers must demonstrate a minimum of ten years of continuous experience providing project management software services to public agencies and must comply with FAA General Contract Provisions, County security requirements including background checks for airport access, and California state business registration requirements.
periodOfPerformance: 4-year base term with two 2-year extension options (up to 8 years total)
title: Project Management Software Services for Sacramento County Department of Airports
